oai_dc:dc.dc:title: Parrots - First Time Breeding
oai_dc:dc.dc:creator: Zamora Padrón, Rafael
oai_dc:dc.dc:description: Results in parrot breeding within aviculture are always variable because most species do not begin breeding in the first years of life. Pairs need compatibility, maturity, proper incubation, and skill feeding chicks. This article reports on Loro Parque Fundación’s breeding season and highlights three species reproduced there for the first time: the Yellow-fronted Parrot, Goiás Parakeet, and Rock Parrot. [For the full article, with images, please open the PDF link below]
oai_dc:dc.dc:source: AFA Watchbird; Vol. 52 No. 1 (2025): AFA Watchbird April 2025
